subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the interdependence of the four factors of production in a shifting economy
Evaluate the long-term ecological and economic trade-offs of the Green Revolution
Deconstruct the structural inequalities in rural land and labor markets
Analyze the credit market distortions and the mechanics of the rural debt trap
Assess the role of infrastructure and human capital in rural diversification
Synthesize agricultural surplus theories with capital reinvestment cycles

lesson_plan.title: Theoretical Framework of Production
topics_covered: Advanced analysis of Land, Labour, Physical Capital, and Human Capital
The 'Human Capital' catalyst: Why it is the superior factor
Fixed vs. Working Capital in high-tech agricultural settings
student_activities: Draft a thesis on why Human Capital is essential to activate Land and Capital
Categorize capital for a high-tech organic farm vs. a traditional farm
assessment: Analytical essay on the 'Superiority of Human Capital'
Critical peer review of production plans

lesson_plan.title: Intensive Cultivation & Diminishing Returns
topics_covered: Multiple cropping vs. modern farming: A marginal utility perspective
The role of institutional infrastructure (electricity) in economic shifts
Calculation of yield per hectare using modern vs. traditional inputs
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the 'Law of Diminishing Returns' in fixed land scenarios
student_activities: Calculate the percentage increase in yield from textbook data
Graph the relationship between fertilizer input and total output
assessment: Numerical problem set on agricultural productivity

lesson_plan.title: The Green Revolution: A Cost-Benefit Audit
topics_covered: HYV seed technology and the 'Package' approach
Socio-economic disparities: The widening gap between large and small farmers
Regional imbalance in Indian agricultural growth
teaching_strategy: Debate: 'The Green Revolution: A Boon or a Bane for the Small Farmer?'
student_activities: Interpret complex bar graphs of food grain production over 50 years
Identify the 'Externalities' of modern farming (pollution, health costs)
assessment: Comparative report on regional agricultural disparities

lesson_plan.title: Environmental Sustainability & Natural Capital
topics_covered: Soil fertility degradation and chemical toxicity
Groundwater depletion: The 'Tragedy of the Commons' in Palampur
Scientific reports vs. short-term economic gains
teaching_strategy: Case study analysis of water table fall in North-West India
Discussion on 'Natural Capital' as a finite resource
student_activities: Propose a sustainable 'Master Plan' for water conservation in Palampur
Write a critical analysis on the loss of soil microorganisms due to chemical usage
assessment: Short answer test on environmental externalities

lesson_plan.title: Labour Market Distortions & Wage Theory
topics_covered: Land distribution inequality (Gini coefficient concept)
Why wages remain below government-mandated minimums
The relationship between landlessness and bargaining power
teaching_strategy: Pie chart analysis of land ownership inequality
Lecture on the 'Supply and Demand' of rural labor
student_activities: Construct a detailed pie chart of land distribution in Palampur
Analyze the 'Story of Dala' to understand wage suppression
assessment: Analytical note on 'Bargaining Power in Rural Labour Markets'

lesson_plan.title: Rural Finance: Credit Cycles & Reinvestment
topics_covered: Mechanics of the debt trap for small farmers
Interest rate arbitrage: Moneylenders vs. Banks
Surplus reinvestment cycles for large farmers
teaching_strategy: Flowcharting the 'Vicious Cycle' of debt vs. the 'Virtuous Cycle' of profit
Calculation of compound interest from informal sources
student_activities: Compare the total repayment of Savita (moneylender) vs. a bank loan
Map the reinvestment path of Tejpal Singh’s surplus wheat profits
assessment: Financial problem set on rural credit costs

lesson_plan.title: Diversification & The Multiplier Effect
topics_covered: Non-farm activities: Dairy, Transport, and IT as growth engines
The 'Multiplier Effect' of infrastructure (roads, electricity) on village GDP
Barriers to entry for small-scale rural manufacturing
teaching_strategy: Mind mapping the 'Multiplier Effect' of education (Kareem’s center)
Summary synthesis of the entire chapter
student_activities: Develop a 'Strategic Development Plan' for Palampur's non-farm sector
Participate in a high-level chapter-end synthesis quiz
assessment: Comprehensive essay test (30 marks) on 'The Future of Rural India'

teaching_aids: Complex data sets on Indian agriculture and land distribution
Case study handouts on Savita and Tejpal Singh
teacher_tip: Challenge students to find the link between 'Marketable Surplus' and the ability to innovate; focus on why capital concentration in few hands limits the growth of the overall village economy.
